lines: The dead man carried a road warden's tin note case.

His note is old field advice: ship ash can stay warm for decades; do not pray over unknown relic metal in a crowd; leave clean water at quarantine posts; never let children collect bright fragments after a fall.

His last entry is dated three days back, in a worse hand: 'They keep something alive past the low wall down here. Bound to a post, not yet on a cross. Saving it, they said, for after. I am not going to look at it again. Neither should you.'
And, practical to the end: if a chapel bell rings with no hand on the rope, sleep somewhere else.
